free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of 16O and 18O ions
from 64Zn at energies near the Coulomb barrier
The electrostatic accelerator at the
University of Sao Paulo.
The energy resolution, essentially due to
the target thickness
The targets were made by vacuum evaporation of ZnO,
enriched to 99.85% in 64Zn, on thin layer (~ 5
mu-g/cm2) of Au evaporated onto 15 mu-g/cm2 carbon
backing. The 64Zn targets were typically ~ 10 mu-g/cm2.
Beam currents measured in a Faraday cup after
the target were of the order of 200 nA.
The scattered ions were detected at ANG.lab
~174.0 degr. in a cooled (~-30.C) annular silicon
surface-barrier detector.
Two silicon detectors symmetrically placed with
respect to the beam direction at ANG.lab = 30.0
degr. were used to normalize the cross sections to
absolute values and to monitor the beam direction.
For the angular distribution measurements, a
split-pole magnetic spectrograph was employed. The
scattered ions were detected in a gas-filled E-dE
position sensitive detector with a delay line readout,
placed in the focal plane of the spectrograph.
The values of the first excite states compiler took
from NUDAT. In the article values are absent.
Data were digitized from figs. of the ref.
SD
VS. Corrections in AUTHOR.
